pages.full_text:
We spent the afternoon at the campsite enjoying the view + the sunshine. Simon has not brought any maps [?Ordnance Survey] because we can now rely on his mob.... but alas he has forgotten to bring the lead for his mob. Fortunately when we went to the library - it has finally opened after months of lockdown - he got out a book of walks on the Gower so we can use that.

A lovely sunset.

Sunday
    We set off on an even longer walk of 6 miles to see the metal light house (1865, the last metal light house in Europe.) Parts of it are made of copper, mined locally. We drove to [?Llan-]mador + walked from there. Dunes, lovely pine woods idyllic walking. Shells on the beach, bright sunshine. We reached the light house, Whiteford Bay, at 11.35., Five minutes after low tide, which gave us confidence. The beach to it was rocky + sloshy.
    After we got back to the van, we bought ice creams, my first since Thorpeness last summer.
pages.notes: The mention of 'lockdown' and a library reopening after 'months of lockdown' strongly suggests this entry dates to 2020, which would fall outside the stated volume range of 2003-2019; the volume may extend into 2020. No year is explicitly written on the page. 'Wan-mador' or '[?Llan-]mador' is likely Llanmadoc on the Gower Peninsula, from which walkers reach Whiteford Burrows lighthouse. The word after 'maps' is partially legible; likely 'Ordnance Survey' but could read differently. 'Thorpeness' is a village in Suffolk, England.

pages.full_text: On the walk we saw a mare separated from her foal by a fence. I telephoned a [?mussel] number on a gate of a sheep field + got the
pages.notes: Page is largely blank below the first three lines; the text is a continuation from the left page. The word transcribed as '[?mussel]' is partially legible — could be 'council' or another word; it appears to be an adjective or type describing the phone number found on a gate. The sentence continues onto a subsequent page (text ends mid-sentence with 'got the').
